headline: A US export-control order forcing Anthropic to pull Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominated the quarter as inference-speed records and Model Context Protocol broke out.
body: A government directive citing export-control concerns and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability removed Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 from availability, including across Europe, drawing repeated coverage on the scope and durability of the ban. In parallel, two systems crossed 1,000 tokens per second: Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma, which applies diffusion techniques to text generation, and Xiaomi and TileRT's trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model using FP4 quantization and speculative decoding. Model Context Protocol entered as a new technique, with IBM tying it to design systems so agents can build consistent applications. Tool use led the rotation, alongside selective model routing, local inference, and prompt caching.
